insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change provides more flexibility in querying data and can be useful when building custom storefronts or integrating with other sales channels. Developers should review their existing code and update it to take advantage of this new feature. The Storefront API documentation should be consulted for more information on using the @inContext directive with the channelId argument.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants may see improved performance and more accurate data in their custom storefronts, as developers can now query data specific to their sales channels.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and control over data querying, allowing developers to build more customized and integrated storefronts. It also enables merchants to have more accurate and relevant data in their sales channels.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test against new inventory APIs.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirements have been updated to include using a unique name for apps to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports channelId, allowing for channel-specific context in queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: Fields productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle have been added to ExchangeLineItem.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1 reads disabled command metadata and preserves actionable build errors during h2 deploy.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects and uses jsconfig.json for JavaScript projects.

recap.headline: Shopify updates GraphQL Admin API, Storefront API, and CLI with new features and requirements

themes.title: API Updates
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the Storefront API `@inContext` directive now supports `channelId`. Additionally, new fields have been added to `ExchangeLineItem` and discount allocations are available for bundle components in POS UI Extensions.

themes.title: Security and Authentication
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and apps that store cardholder data must be updated to use this certificate.

themes.title: Development Tools and Best Practices
themes.detail: The Shopify CLI has been updated to version 4.5.2, and @shopify/cli-hydrogen has been updated to version 13.0.2. Additionally, the App Store requirements have been updated to require unique app names.

actions.text: Enable physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate for card deposit endpoint
Review and update app names to comply with new App Store requirements
